title: Human Longevity Launches $599 Clinical-Grade Whole Genome Test
description: Human Longevity, Inc. unveiled a $599 clinical-grade whole genome sequencing report on the anniversary of founder J. Craig Venter's legacy, paired with AI-driven disease prevention insights. Price compression of this magnitude pushes clinical genomics into mainstream consumer longevity protocols and validates Venter's bet that genome-first prevention would scale.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Pre-Festival Buildout Confirms 150+ Sessions
description: Final program for Vitalist Bay opens Thursday May 14 at Berkeley's Lighthaven, now confirmed at 100+ speakers and 150+ workshops across four days. Daily startup pitch sessions for early-stage longevity companies and 18 onsite health tests turn the venue into a working clinical-experience venue, not just a conference.

title: Cash-Pay Longevity Cohort Invisible to Major Health Data Platforms
description: Inexion analysis shows that despite $554M in Q4 2025 longevity biotech funding (a 900% jump over Q3), no major real-world-evidence platform — IQVIA, Komodo, Symphony — captures cash-pay longevity practices. The structural data gap is now blocking pharma HEOR modeling for senolytic, GLP-1, and biomarker-driven longevity indications.

title: AFAR Aging-US Meeting Report Formalizes Geroscience Pivot to Reversal
description: Aging-US Volume 18 published the 12th ARDD meeting report, with key presentations explicitly testing whether biological age can be reversed via partial cellular reprogramming. The synthesis points toward organ-wide or whole-body rejuvenation as a credible near-term horizon, marking field-level consensus that intervention has moved beyond observation.

title: Longevity News Roundup Week 19 Highlights Multi-Target Alzheimer's, Glycation
description: Longevity.Technology's weekly roundup spotlights Estée Lauder's mechanistic data linking sugar-induced glycation to skin cell senescence and systemic inflammation, plus Scribe Therapeutics' STX-1150 CRISPR program for Parkinson's. The convergence reinforces that aging-relevant interventions are spreading across consumer, cosmetic, and CNS verticals simultaneously.

title: Human Longevity Launches $599 Clinical-Grade Whole Genome Sequencing
description: Human Longevity, Inc. announced a $599 clinical-grade whole genome sequencing report with AI-enhanced insights for proactive disease prevention, honoring founder J. Craig Venter's legacy.

title: Longevity News Roundup — Week 19, 2026
description: Phil Newman and Nina Patrick examine multi-target Alzheimer's therapy, sugar glycation in aging, personalized CRISPR for Parkinson's, FDA real-time trials, and peptide platforms.

title: ARDD 12th Meeting Report — Toward Actionable Interventions in Human Aging
description: Aging-US Volume 18 publishes the ARDD synthesis: epigenome as central regulator, partial cellular reprogramming, and systemic effects pointing to organ-wide rejuvenation.

title: Inexion: $554M Q4 Longevity Funding Yet RWE Data Gap Persists
description: Q4 2025 longevity biotech funding hit $554M (900% above Q3), but no major claims-first data platform reaches the cash-pay longevity cohort, blocking pharma HEOR modeling.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Set to Spotlight Longevity's Next Phase
description: Four-day Vitalist Bay festival at Lighthaven, Berkeley brings together researchers, founders, clinicians and investors across biotechnology, AI x Bio, biostasis, and health optimization.

title: BioAge Labs R&D Day: BGE-102 Phase 1 Shows Big hsCRP Drops
description: BioAge presented Phase 1 results for BGE-102 with 24-hour coverage at 60 mg once-daily dosing and profound hsCRP reductions in obese subjects, setting up cardiovascular and DME POC studies.

title: Creatine Shows Synergy With Exercise in Older Adults
description: Spanish researchers in Experimental Gerontology showed creatine combined with power training improved BDNF, antioxidant defense, inflammation, and lower-limb function in 103 older adults over 16 weeks.

title: May 2026 Clinical Studies in Aging Roundup
description: Active studies include everolimus mTORC1 inhibition (NCT05835999), RAP PAC rapalog pharmacology (NCT05949658), and a GLP-1 trial in postmenopausal women evaluating aging biomarkers.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Returns to Berkeley May 14-17
description: The Vitalism Foundation's pop-up longevity festival returns to Lighthaven Berkeley May 14-17 with 60+ speakers across Biotech, Investors, Replacement, Longevity Science, AI x Bio, and Biostasis.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets GPX4 in Ageing Cells
description: Gil and Tate groups identify chloroacetamide senolytics that selectively kill senescent cells by inhibiting GPX4 and triggering ferroptosis, with data across melanoma, prostate, and ovarian models.

title: Rubedo Reports Positive Phase 1 Results for RLS-1496
description: First-in-class topical GPX4 modulator was well tolerated with dose-related target engagement, senescent-cell reduction, lower inflammatory cytokines, and ~20% reduction in epidermal thickness.

title: Academic Clinical Trials for Rapamycin Aim to Answer Dosing Questions
description: Multi-phase academic rapamycin trial will run an RCT of ~84 older adults comparing daily, intermittent, and placebo regimens to establish evidence-based dosing for healthy aging use.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Sign $2.75B AI Drug Discovery Pact
description: Lilly grants Insilico $115M upfront with up to $2.75B in milestones plus tiered royalties for exclusive worldwide license to a pipeline of oral preclinical assets.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup April 2026
description: Monthly digest: replacement-over-repair strategy, inflammaging-epigenetic clock links, engineered stem-cell antibody factories, SRN-901 lifespan extension, BCLXL-PROTAC senolytics in COPD.

title: NYT Magazine: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: Long-form feature on partial reprogramming, Altos Labs, and the first safety trial of rejuvenation therapy in humans targeting glaucoma.

title: The Longevity Trade Is No Longer a Buzzword — $120B Market
description: Markets Insider frames longevity as a $120B market following FDA clearance of the first cellular rejuvenation trial, with GLP-1 receptor agonists labeled the first formal class of longevity therapeutics.

title: Nature Daily Briefing: First Human Trial of Cellular Age Reversal
description: Partial reprogramming clinical trial set for 2026 will use targeted protein expression to reverse cellular ageing in glaucoma patients — the first such test in humans.

title: Junyue Cao on How the Body Ages, Cell by Cell
description: Rockefeller researcher discusses ultra-high-throughput single-cell technologies applied to mapping the cellular hallmarks of aging across tissues.

title: How Intestinal Aging Encourages Harmful Bacteria
description: An Aging Cell study elucidates the bidirectional relationship between intestinal epithelial aging and age-related microbiome shifts that favor pathogenic species.

title: Thinking AI Outperforms Human Doctors on Real-Life Clinical Data
description: New benchmark shows reasoning-capable LLMs outperform human physicians on real clinical decision tasks, with implications for AI-assisted longevity medicine and diagnostics.

title: Q1 2026 Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B Across 49 Deals
description: DLT dataset projects 2026 capital flows of $8-9B (55-60% YoY growth), with median deal size of $21.8M signaling a maturing pipeline.

title: Longevity Startup Funding 2025-2026 — Outlier-Driven Signal
description: Analysis shows a large share of 2026 longevity funding signal is driven by outlier rounds like Insilico-Lilly and WHOOP, masking softer median-stage trends.

title: Insilico Forms Longevity Board to Oversee AI Aging Research
description: Insilico Medicine establishes a dedicated longevity scientific advisory board to direct AI-driven discovery within its aging-focused pipeline.

title: Inbrain Completes Enrollment in First Human Graphene Brain Interface Study
description: First-in-human study of graphene-based neural interfaces fully enrolled, supporting future applications in age-related neurodegeneration.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Extends Median Lifespan 33% in Aged Mice
description: SRN-901 multipathway longevity drug treatment of 18-month-old mice produced a 33% increase in median remaining lifespan plus reductions in frailty and tumor incidence.
